changes_made: Changed k8s_networkpolicy_encryption_in_transit_enabled to k8s_networkpolicy_tls_enforced for clarity and consistency
review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the control 'CCCS SC-8: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity'. The naming conventions mostly follow the pattern, but a slight adjustment was made for clarity and consistency with existing naming standards. The function names are descriptive and specific, indicating clear pass/fail conditions.
naming_improvements: Use 'tls_enforced' instead of 'encryption_in_transit_enabled' for network policies to align with common terminology
title: CCCS SC-8: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
step1_reasoning: The control 'CCCS SC-8: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity' focuses on ensuring data is transmitted securely, which maps to ensuring TLS is used for ingress traffic and mutual TLS (mTLS) is enabled within service meshes. Additionally, NetworkPolicies can be configured to enforce encryption in transit within the cluster.
